arts.ttl: Breaking: Bayern Munich star Aleksandar Pavlović exits VfB Stuttgart match with early injury
arts.sum: Bayern Munich midfielder Aleksandar Pavlović was forced to leave today’s game due to injury in the eight minute after being low-bridged by VfB Stuttgart’s Fabian Rieder. Luckily for Bayern Munich coach Vincent Kompany, the central midfield is one of the strongest areas of the squad with Pavlović, Palhinha, Kimmich, Leon Goretzka, and Konrad Laimer all populating the depth chart at the position.

arts.ttl: Joshua Kimmich makes 400th Bayern Munich appearance
arts.sum: A historic milestone for Joshua Kimmich. Starting against Stuttgart, the 29-year-old made his 400th Bayern Munich appearance since his move to the Rekordmeister from tonight’s opposition in 2015. Since making…
The lineups for Saturday's Bundesliga clash between Bayern Munich and VfB Stuttgart have been announced.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich vs VfB Stuttgart: Gamethread and Live Blog
arts.sum: With the international break now over, Bayern Munich are back in action versus VfB Stuttgart, and are looking to prove that the winless streak is a thing of the past. While you’re waiting for the game, why not check out our preview podcast?

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich loanee Frans Krätzig ready for Allianz Arena return
arts.sum: Bayern Munich loanee Frans Krätzig is enjoying his time with VfB Stuttgart so far, but is looking forward to returning to the Allianz Arena on Saturday. Sa far, Krätzig has one assist in three games playing as a left-back for VfB Stuttgart.

arts.ttl: “Remarkable” VfB Stuttgart powered by Bayern Munich loanee Alexander Nübel
arts.sum: One of the players who has helped VfB Stuttgart become a force in the Bundesliga is Bayern Munich loanee Alexander Nübel. Bayern Munich board member for sport Max Eberl admires the work that VfB Stuttgart is doing from afar.

arts.ttl: Stuttgart boss blown away by Leweling's development
arts.sum: Stuttgart boss Fabian Wohlgemuth has described how special he believes the development of Jamie Leweling has been.

arts.ttl: “I hope Harry wins trophies this year”: Former Bayern Munich CEO on Harry Kane vs Robert Lewandowski
arts.sum: In a long interview with kicker, former Bayern Munich CEO Karl-Heinz Rummenigge was asked about the upcoming clash against FC Barcelona, specifically a comparison between Harry Kane and their current striker — Robert Lewandowski. “He plays well, he scores goals, he looks great on FC Bayern and we are happy that he’s playing here.

arts.ttl: Report: Bayern's contract talks with Davies on hold
arts.sum: Bayern Munich have reportedly put their contract extension talks with Alphonso Davies on hold until further notice, as they are unwilling to meet his wage demands.

arts.ttl: Despite being hurt by Jonathan Tah, Bayern Munich’s Aleksandar Pavlović is ready to face VfB Stuttgart
arts.sum: The bandage on the Bayern Munich player’s knee remains, but his fitness has now improved to the extent that he will be ready to start in Bayern’s weekend match against VfB Stuttgart, according to a report captured by @iMiaSanMia : Considering Pavlović finally managed to make his starting debut for Germany against Netherlands on Monday, it seems likely that this report is correct and that the Bayern Campus graduate will similarly be ready to start on the weekend.
